sections.paragraph:
περικαθίζω fut. 2 sg. περικαθιεῖς Dt 20:12 (Diod S 20, 103, 5 Fischer v.l.; Mitt-Wilck I/2, 11 B. Fgm. a, 10 [123 b.c.]; LXX; Jos., Ant. 13, 151; TestJud 9:4, but mostly in a hostile sense = ‘besiege’) sit around (Maximus Tyr. 21, 6d περικαθίσαντες ἐν κύκλῳ τῇ πυρᾷ) Lk 22:55 D.—DELG s.v. ἕζομαι. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Maximus Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 801
